reconciled.rationale_completion: Both teachers within 4s of each other (138 and 142). Average of 140 is the consensus for this peak performance.

phase_timings.rationale: GPT-4o saw an unusually fast suture_placement (19s) and an extreme third_throw (51s) — possibly attributing extra reinforcing manipulation to the third throw rather than to a separate cutting/setup phase. Claude's 24s third throw and 30s cut split the same time window differently. Averaging gives a more defensible split.

penalties.rationale_deviation: Average of (2.5 + 3.0) / 2 = 2.75. Both teachers low confidence on exact mm.

training_signal.note: Third dual-teacher comparison. Both teachers agree this is a peak performance — complete closure, surgeon's knot, hand alternation, low penalties, sub-145s completion. The 3.5-point score delta is the smallest in the series. As with post2, the throw boundaries remain noisy between the two teachers but the totals converge tightly. GPT-4o has a tendency to lump 'extra reinforcing manipulation' into the third throw phase, inflating it dramatically (51s vs Claude's 24s). This is a useful, repeatable systematic difference worth flagging for the critique agent.
training_signal.systematic_finding: GPT-4o consistently inflates the third_throw duration relative to Claude (post2: 46.2 vs 30; postV7: 51 vs 24; postV8: 67 vs 30). This appears to be a phase-attribution difference, not an actual disagreement on what happened in the video — GPT-4o is rolling extra manipulation/setup into the throw label while Claude separates them out.

notes: Third teacher comparison after post2 and postV8. The pattern across all three: total time agrees within 4-8s, total FLS agrees within 3.5-17.5 points, but throw-phase splits diverge significantly — particularly the third throw, which GPT-4o systematically inflates by 20-30s vs Claude. This is now confirmed as a reproducible systematic difference, not noise.
